pi_items.source: IMO Convention on the International Regulations for Preventing Collisions at Sea, 1972 (COLREGs). Verified via multiple sources including ecolregs.com and US CFR 33 § 83.10

Dover Strait TSS configuration confirmed via marinepublic.com blog post which states 'South West Lane is for vessels travelling down Channel' (heading Southwest = 225 degrees). Standard nautical compass confirmed via Wikipedia Points of the Compass.

Dover Strait TSS configuration confirmed via marinepublic.com blog post which states 'North East Lane is for vessels which are travelling up to the North Sea areas' (heading Northeast = 45 degrees). Standard nautical compass confirmed via Wikipedia Points of the Compass.

pi_items.verified_value: [20, 70] is appropriate for a Northeast-heading rogue vessel
pi_items.source: Standard compass navigation: Northeast = 45 degrees. Range [20, 70] encompasses 45 degrees and allows ±25 degrees variation, which is reasonable for a violating vessel not maintaining strict lane discipline.

pi_items.verified_value: [200, 250] is appropriate for a Southwest-heading compliant vessel
pi_items.source: Standard compass navigation: Southwest = 225 degrees. Range [200, 250] encompasses 225 degrees and allows ±25 degrees variation, which is reasonable for a properly-disciplined vessel following the lane.

pi_items.verified_value: /opt/bridgecommand is the correct installation path for Bridge Command (built from GitHub source)
pi_items.source: Environment setup scripts confirm Bridge Command is built from https://github.com/bridgecommand/bc and installed to /opt/bridgecommand. Verified via GitHub repository and bridgecommand.co.uk documentation.

pi_items.verified_value: Path is syntactically valid and will be created during task execution
pi_items.source: Setup scripts show Documents directory structure is created as part of task initialization (setup_task.sh creates /home/ga/Documents).

privileged_info_summary: The task simulates a realistic Dover Strait Traffic Separation Scheme (TSS) scenario where a merchant vessel violates established maritime law. COLREGS Rule 10 governs traffic separation schemes, requiring vessels in a TSS to proceed in the appropriate lane in the general direction of traffic flow. The Dover Strait TSS has two opposite lanes: the Southwest lane for traffic heading toward the Atlantic (bearing 225 degrees) and the Northeast lane for traffic heading toward the North Sea (bearing 45 degrees). The metadata correctly specifies these bearings, with reasonable heading ranges around the centerline headings (±25 degrees variation). The scenario will be created synthetically using Bridge Command, an open-source maritime training simulator, allowing configuration of vessel positions, headings, and speeds to represent both the rogue violating vessel (heading Northeast 20-70 degrees range) and the compliant vessel (heading Southwest 200-250 degrees range).
